math: 1200 B/event. b64 typ 15000*1200/1e9 = 0.018 GB/day -> $2.74/mo. b32 typ 200*1200/1e9 = 0.00024 GB/day -> $0.04/mo. Total typ = $2.77/mo computed from the summed event rate (15200*1200/1e9*30.4*5.00); the per-rule-rounded figures sum to $2.78, a sub-cent rounding-order artifact (the machine field total uses $2.77).

naive_wrong_model.dedup_wrong: A model that sees two identical-except-arch rules and DEDUPS them counts only b64, dropping the b32 (32-bit) coverage; reports $2.74/mo and silently loses 32-bit auditing.
naive_wrong_model.double_count_wrong: A model that treats arch as irrelevant and double-counts the SAME execve volume twice over-states (would count b64 volume x2 = $5.48/mo). Correct: SUM the two DISTINCT arch slices (b64 + small b32) = ~$2.77/mo.
